title: The Isle

notes: Played multiple servers for approx. 8-10 hours, FPS was on-par with Windows 10, maybe just a little FPS loss on Linux compared to Windows but that's expected.
title: The Isle

Fails to launch with error -->

title: The Isle

notes: Runs perfect on Ubuntu 18.04LTS, have not tested on Gentoo yet. If you have an Nvidia card, you NEED to install the proprietary drivers nvidia-drivers, not the open source Nouveau drivers. It is also a requirement by steam to be running Nouveau. I wanted to add this in, because two other comments on here with the borked rating are running Nouveau.
title: The Isle

notes: Everything was tested except connecting to servers hosted by others.  Other than the Wine D3D11 tweak, everything seemed to work just fine.  The only notable issue is that the quality settings for the graphics seemed to have no major effect on FPS (all settings produced around 20 FPS).  This included even going all the way up to Cinematic.  There might have also been some color distortion, but nothing that made the game unplayable.
title: The Isle

notes: Everything was tested (except all the dinos).  There's minor graphical glitches with some shadows, but you'd only see them when looking for them.  (Moire patterns forming on trees in the distance.)  I played with everything on Cinematic and got a 30 FPS on average.  (Latest proton and nvidia drivers really make a difference in how good the quality of the game is.)
title: The Isle

notes: Played for several hours, only problem is random fps drops. They happen sometimes. 70 fps stable on low settings overall.
title: The Isle

notes: Consistent high 40's/low 50's fps on Cinematic quality. Very stable and enjoyable.
title: The Isle

notes: Works perfectly out of the box with good performances. The only side way is the loading screen not deplaying.
title: The Isle

notes: Booted this up in multiplayer with a friend and after updating my graphics driver (it's a new-ish system and I forgot to do so originally) it performed exceptionally
title: The Isle
